item.title: TPIA Request: CCSO Records — Former Deputy Tomas Ramirez — May 26, 2026
item.description:
Transmitted via email to Sheriff Justin Lindemann and Eugenia Behrens, Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, May 26, 2026. CC: J. Eric Magee (Allison Bass & Magee), Jay Johannes and Carolyn Olson (Colorado County).

TO: Sheriff Justin Lindemann / Eugenia Behrens, Colorado County Sheriff’s Office eugenia.behrens@co.colorado.tx.us | justin.lindemann@co.colorado.tx.us

CC: e.magee@allison-bass.com | jay.johannes@co.colorado.tx.us | carolyn.olson@co.colorado.tx.us

FROM: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 26, 2026

RE: Texas Public Information Act Request — Records Concerning Former Deputy Tomas Ramirez

Sheriff Lindemann:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, Tex. Gov’t Code Chapter 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records held by the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office concerning former CCSO Deputy Tomas Ramirez and the conduct alleged to have occurred in or about September 2024 that resulted in his prosecution under Tex. Penal Code § 39.04 and deferred adjudication community supervision in or about 2025. Without limiting the foregoing, I specifically request:

item.title: TPIA Request: Court Records — Criminal Case of Tomas Ramirez — May 26, 2026
item.description:
Transmitted via email to Valerie Harmon, Colorado County District Clerk, May 26, 2026.

TO: Valerie Harmon, Colorado County District Clerk 400 Spring Street, Room 103-B, Columbus, Texas 78934 valerie.harmon@co.colorado.tx.us

FROM: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 26, 2026

RE: Request for Court Records — Criminal Case of Tomas Ramirez (former CCSO Deputy)

Dear Ms. Harmon:

Pursuant to the common-law and constitutional rights of public access to court records, Tex. R. Jud. Admin. 12, and to the extent applicable Tex. Gov’t Code Chapter 552 — including § 552.022(a)(17) (information also contained in a public court record is public information) — I respectfully request copies of all publicly available records in the criminal case(s) of Tomas Ramirez, former Colorado County Sheriff’s Office deputy, arising from conduct alleged to have occurred in or about September 2024 and resulting in deferred adjudication community supervision in or about 2025.

item.title: TPIA Request: Texas Ranger Investigation of Tomas Ramirez — DPS — May 26, 2026
item.description:
Transmitted via email to Texas Department of Public Safety, Office of General Counsel — Public Information, May 26, 2026.

TO: Texas Department of Public Safety — Office of General Counsel, Public Information 5805 N. Lamar Boulevard, Austin, Texas 78752-4422 openrecords@dps.texas.gov

FROM: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 26, 2026

RE: Texas Public Information Act Request — Texas Ranger Investigation of Tomas Ramirez

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, Tex. Gov’t Code Chapter 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records held by the Texas Department of Public Safety concerning the investigation of former Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Deputy Tomas Ramirez and the conduct alleged to have occurred in or about September 2024 at the Colorado County Jail. Without limiting the foregoing, I specifically request:

item.title: Demand Letter: Matagorda County Failure to Respond — Bannert Employment Records — May 24, 2026
item.description:
Transmitted via email to rdeleon@co.matagorda.tx.us, CC: msloan@co.matagorda.tx.us (County Attorney), May 24, 2026.

TO: Matagorda County — Custodian of Records rdeleon@co.matagorda.tx.us

CC: Matthew Hardy Sloan, Matagorda County Attorney msloan@co.matagorda.tx.us

FROM: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 24, 2026

RE: Failure to Respond — Public Information Act Request Submitted May 2, 2026 — Faith Marie Bannert Employment Records

I submitted a Texas Public Information Act request to Matagorda County on May 2, 2026, via email to rdeleon@co.matagorda.tx.us. The subject of the request was employment records for Faith Marie Bannert, a former Jailer who held a Temporary Jailer License and worked at Matagorda County from approximately December 6, 2023 through March 19, 2024.

item.title: OAG Complaint: Matagorda County Failure to Respond — §552.3215 — May 24, 2026
item.description:
Filed with the Texas Attorney General, Open Government Division, May 24, 2026, concurrent with demand letter to Matagorda County.

TO: Texas Attorney General — Open Government Division P.O. Box 12548, Austin, TX 78711-2548 open.government@oag.texas.gov

FROM: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 24, 2026

RE: Open Government Complaint — Matagorda County — Failure to Respond to TPIA Request

Complaint #

This complaint is submitted against Matagorda County for failure to respond to a Texas Public Information Act request within the statutory period, in violation of Tex. Gov’t Code §552.228.

item.title: Before Colorado County Asks for More Money, Explain the $2.2 Million Already Spent
item.description:
Published as a letter to the editor in the Colorado County Citizen, May 12, 2026.

On Monday, April 27, the Colorado County Commissioners Court approved an architectural assessment for a proposed new justice center. According to reporting in this paper, the jail recently passed inspection but has rust documented since 2024, out-of-compliance sliding doors, no space for additional female inmates, only one psych cell, and no room for sheriff’s office staff growth.

item.title: CCSO Invoice for May 2, 2026 Bannert Records Request — May 12, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Eugenia Behrens, Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, May 12, 2026.

FROM: Eugenia Behrens (eugenia.behrens@co.colorado.tx.us) — Colorado County Sheriff’s Office TO: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 12, 2026

SUBJECT: May 2, 2026 Response

The Colorado County Sheriff’s Office transmitted an invoice for costs associated with responding to the May 2, 2026 TPIA request for Faith Marie Bannert employment records. The response stated that records would not be released until payment is received, with options for pick-up in person or mailing a money order or cashier’s check.

item.title: AG Complaint: CCSO Claimed 'No Records' for Employees Named in a State Investigation
item.description:
Submitted to the Texas Office of the Attorney General, Open Government Division, May 8, 2026. OAG Complaint No. OR-26-024051-IC.

██████████████████ ██████████████████████████████ ████████████████, TX █████ ████████████████ ██████████████████████████████

May 2, 2026

VIA ONLINE SUBMISSION / EMAIL

Office of the Attorney General of Texas Open Government Division P.O. Box 12548 Austin, Texas 78711

Re: Complaint — False “No Responsive Records” Response Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Request Dated April 23, 2026 Response Dated April 30, 2026

item.title: OAG Complaint Filed: OR-26-024049-IC — False No-Records Response for Smith and Flores Personnel Records — May 8, 2026
item.description:
Complaint filed with the Texas Attorney General Open Government Division, May 8, 2026, via online submission.

OAG Reference: OR-26-024049-IC Governmental Body: Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Subject of Complaint: False “No Responsive Records” response to TPIA request for Lt. Holly Smith and Jailer Perla Flores personnel records

A complaint was filed with the Texas Attorney General’s Office of Open Government alleging that the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office issued a false “No Responsive Records” response to the April 23, 2026 TPIA request for personnel records of Lt. Holly Smith and Jailer Perla Flores — both of whom were directly involved in the January 19, 2026 use-of-force incident at the Colorado County Jail.

item.title: CCSO Open Records Response — Eugenia Behrens — April 23, 2026 Response — May 7, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Eugenia Behrens, Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, May 7, 2026.

FROM: Eugenia Behrens (eugenia.behrens@co.colorado.tx.us) — Colorado County Sheriff’s Office TO: ████████████████████████████ DATE: May 7, 2026

SUBJECT: April 23, 2026 Response

The Colorado County Sheriff’s Office transmitted a response to the outstanding open records request, providing the April 23, 2026 response document.

“Response attached.”

Attachment received:

  • 04_23_2026 Response.pdf

Correspondence #

Primary email correspondence for this record.

item.title: Colorado County Open Records Response — Angela Ford — May 5, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Angela Ford, Legal Secretary, Colorado County, May 5, 2026, CC: Carolyn Olson.

FROM: Angela Ford (angela.ford@co.colorado.tx.us) — Legal Secretary, Colorado County TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: Carolyn Olson (carolyn.olson@co.colorado.tx.us) DATE: May 5, 2026

Colorado County Legal Secretary Angela Ford transmitted a response to an outstanding open records request, providing copies of correspondence letters to the Texas Attorney General and a basic incident report.

Attachments received:

  • 04.09.2026_Ltr_AG Opinion.pdf (two copies)
  • 04.10.2026_Ltr_AG Opinion.pdf
  • Basic Report 20260038.pdf

RESPONSE

item.title: TPIA Request: Employment Records — Faith Marie Bannert
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, May 2, 2026.

May 2, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Custodian of Records 304 Spring Street Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Employment Records, Faith Marie Bannert

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, Tex. Gov’t Code Chapter 552, I hereby request the following public records pertaining to Faith Marie Bannert, who served as a Jailer at the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office from February 14, 2025 through February 7, 2026 under a Temporary Jailer License:

item.title: TPIA Request: Faith Marie Bannert Employment Records — Matagorda County — May 2, 2026
item.description:
Submitted via email to Matagorda County, May 2, 2026.

FROM: ████████████████████████████ TO: rdeleon@co.matagorda.tx.us (Matagorda County) DATE: May 2, 2026

SUBJECT: TPIA Request — Faith Marie Bannert Employment Records

A Texas Public Information Act request was submitted to Matagorda County for employment records related to Faith Marie Bannert, who held a Temporary Jailer License and worked as a Jailer from December 6, 2023 through March 19, 2024.

Records requested:

  1. All personnel records for Faith Marie Bannert, including but not limited to her application for employment, any background check results, hiring documentation, and any records related to her separation from employment.
  2. Any complaints, grievances, or disciplinary records involving Faith Marie Bannert during her employment.
  3. Any internal investigation records or reports involving Faith Marie Bannert.
  4. Any records related to Faith Marie Bannert’s Temporary Jailer License, including documentation submitted to or received from the Texas Commission on Law Enforcement (TCOLE).
  5. Any records related to Faith Marie Bannert’s departure, termination, resignation, or non-renewal.

Fee waiver requested pursuant to Texas Government Code §552.267.

item.title: OAG eFiling: Rebuttal Brief — County Auditor Insurance TPIA — April 23, 2026
item.description:
Rebuttal brief filed with the Texas Attorney General on April 23, 2026, responding to Allison, Bass & Magee’s 15-day letters on the County Auditor liability insurance TPIA request. $7.50 payment processed.

OAG Tracking ID: OR26020676 Texas.gov Request ID: 66404264 PIC ID: 04062026 Governmental Body: Colorado County Received (CTZ): April 23, 2026

Files uploaded:

  • AG_Rebuttal_Brief_v2.docx.pdf
  • Letter to Requestor - Colorado County Request.pdf
  • 20260422_Packet_Auditor_15Day.pdf
  • 20260422_JEM_AG_Auditor_15Day.pdf

Payment: $7.50 — Trace Number 302OR70422010

Correspondence #

Primary email correspondence for this record.

item.title: OAG Ruling Received: OR-26-007348 — Texas DPS (P002659) — April 23, 2026
item.description:
Texas Attorney General ruling received April 23, 2026, regarding a TPIA request to the Texas Department of Public Safety.

OAG Reference: OR-26-007348 Governmental Body: Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) DPS Reference: P002659

The Texas Attorney General issued a ruling in response to the Texas Department of Public Safety’s request for guidance regarding the outstanding TPIA request.

item.title: Texas DPS TPIA Response — P002861 — Records Withheld Per OAG Ruling 2026-0002467 — April 23, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from txdps@govqa.us, April 23, 2026.

FROM: txdps@govqa.us (Texas Department of Public Safety — Office of General Counsel) TO: ████████████████████████████ DATE: April 23, 2026

SUBJECT: RE: P002861-020226

DPS Reference: P002861 (filed February 2, 2026)

The Texas Department of Public Safety responded to the TPIA request filed February 2, 2026, withholding records pursuant to Texas Attorney General Ruling 2026-0002467. The request concerned records related to a use-of-force incident at the Colorado County Jail.

item.title: TPIA Request: Personnel Records — Lt. Holly Smith & Jailer Perla Flores
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, April 23, 2026. Acknowledged received same day by Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens.

April 23, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Custodian of Records 2215 Walnut St, Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Personnel Records, Lt. Holly Smith and Jailer Perla Flores

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, Tex. Gov’t Code Chapter 552, I hereby request the following public records for Lieutenant Holly Smith and Jailer Perla Flores, both employees of the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office:

item.title: Allison Bass 15-Day Letter: County Auditor Insurance TPIA (Second Round) — April 22, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 22, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 22, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Auditor regarding liability insurance coverage

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a second 15-day letter to the Texas Attorney General regarding the TPIA request to the County Auditor for liability insurance coverage records, with copies sent to the requestor.

item.title: TAC Third-Party Notice: Texas Association of Counties Correspondence — April 22, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from pia@county.org, April 22, 2026.

FROM: pia@county.org (Texas Association of Counties) TO: ████████████████████████████ DATE: April 22, 2026

SUBJECT: Letter to Requestor — Colorado County Request

The Texas Association of Counties (TAC) transmitted correspondence to the requestor indicating that a copy of correspondence had been sent to the Texas Attorney General’s office regarding the outstanding public information request to Colorado County.

“Please see attached copy of correspondence sent to the Texas Attorney General’s office regarding your request for information to Colorado County.”

item.title: Expired Deadline Notice to Prause, Letter to Lowrance, and AG Rebuttal — April 9, 2026
item.description:
Three items transmitted on April 9, 2026: a deadline-expired notice to County Judge Ty Prause, a direct letter to County Auditor Michelle Lowrance, and a rebuttal brief to outside counsel regarding the Auditor insurance TPIA.

Item 1 — Expired Deadline Notice to County Judge Ty Prause #

FROM: ████████████████████████████ TO: Ty Prause (ty.prause@co.colorado.tx.us) CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com), Julia McVey (j.mcvey@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 9, 2026

item.title: Allison Bass 10-Day Letter: County Auditor Insurance TPIA — with TAC Third-Party Notice — April 8, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 8, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 8, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Auditor regarding liability insurance coverage

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a 10-day letter regarding the TPIA request to the County Auditor for liability insurance coverage records. In addition to the requestor packet and the letter to the Texas Attorney General, outside counsel also transmitted a third-party notice to the Texas Association of Counties (TAC).

item.title: Allison Bass Response: County Clerk Insurance TPIA — No Responsive Documents — April 8, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 8, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 8, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Clerk regarding liability insurance coverage

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a “No Responsive Documents” response letter to the TPIA request to the County Clerk for liability insurance coverage records.

item.title: Allison Bass Response: County Judge Insurance TPIA — No Responsive Documents — April 7, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 7, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee. Responded same day.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 7, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Judge dated March 9, 2026 regarding liability insurance coverage

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a “No Responsive Documents” response letter to the March 9, 2026 TPIA request to County Judge Ty Prause for liability insurance coverage records.

item.title: Allison Bass Response: County Treasurer Insurance TPIA — No Responsive Documents — April 7, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 7, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 7, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Treasurer dated April 2, 2026

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a “No Responsive Documents” response letter to the April 2, 2026 TPIA request to the County Treasurer for liability insurance coverage records. The request was received by the County on April 6, 2026.

item.title: Demand Letter for Liability Insurance Records — April 3, 2026
item.description:
Sent via email to County Judge Ty Prause, J. Eric Magee, and Julia McVey of Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, April 3, 2026.

FROM: ████████████████████████████ TO: Ty Prause (ty.prause@co.colorado.tx.us), J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com), Julia McVey (j.mcvey@allison-bass.com) DATE: April 3, 2026

SUBJECT: Demand Letter for Insurance

A formal demand letter was transmitted to Colorado County Judge Ty Prause and outside counsel at Allison, Bass & Magee LLP regarding the production of liability insurance coverage records responsive to outstanding TPIA requests.

item.title: OAG eFiling: Rebuttal Briefs for Jail Video and Electronic Communications — April 2–3, 2026
item.description:
Two separate rebuttal briefs filed with the Texas Attorney General on April 2, 2026, responding to Allison, Bass & Magee’s 15-day letters on the jail video and electronic communications TPIA requests. Two $7.50 payments processed.

Submission 1 — Jail Video and Audio Rebuttal #

OAG Tracking ID: OR26016856 Texas.gov Request ID: 66397788 Governmental Body: Colorado County Received (CTZ): April 2, 2026, 8:27:29 PM

item.title: Allison Bass 15-Day Letter: Electronic Communications TPIA — March 31, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, March 31, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee. Responded April 2, 2026 with AG rebuttal brief.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: March 31, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to Colorado County dated March 10, 2026 regarding communications

Outside counsel for Colorado County transmitted a 15-day letter to the Texas Attorney General regarding the March 10, 2026 request for official electronic communications, with copies sent to the requestor.

item.title: Colorado County's PREA Compliance Failure Is a Taxpayer Time Bomb
item.description:
Published as a letter to the editor in the Colorado County Citizen, March 31, 2026.

I am writing to address a critical failure in Colorado County’s governance that threatens both public safety and our fiscal stability. Our county has allocated over $2.2 million in federal State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds (SLFRF) specifically to bring the county jail into compliance. Yet, the jail remains in violation of federal law — specifically the Prison Rape Elimination Act (PREA).

item.title: Allison Bass 15-Day Letter: Jail Video and Audio TPIA — March 30, 2026
item.description:
Received via email from Julia McVey, Paralegal, Allison, Bass & Magee LLP, March 30, 2026, CC: J. Eric Magee. Responded April 2, 2026 with AG rebuttal brief.

FROM: Julia McVey, Paralegal — Allison, Bass & Magee LLP TO: ████████████████████████████ CC: J. Eric Magee (e.magee@allison-bass.com) DATE: March 30, 2026

SUBJECT: ██████████████████ Public Information Act request to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office dated March 9, 2026 regarding video and audio recordings of the incident involving inmate ████████████████ on the evening of January 19, 2026

item.title: TPIA Request: Commissioner's Court Minutes — March 9, 2026
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Clerk, March 10, 2026.

██████████████████ ███████████████ ██████████████████ ████████████████ ████████████████████████████

March 10, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Clerk 400 Spring Street, Suite 103 Columbus, TX 78934

RE: PUBLIC INFORMATION REQUEST PURSUANT TO TEXAS GOVERNMENT CODE CHAPTER 552

Dear County Clerk:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, Texas Government Code Chapter 552, I am requesting copies of the following public records:

RECORDS REQUESTED

  1. The official minutes of the Colorado County Commissioners Court meeting held on March 9, 2026.

item.title: TPIA Request: County Insurance Policy
item.description:
Submitted via email to Colorado County, March 10, 2026. Request submitted as PDF attachment. Sent to County Judge Ty Prause and Sharon Marsalia.

March 10, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Insurance Policy

Request submitted as PDF attachment: TPIA_Request_Insurance_Policy.pdf.

The request sought Colorado County’s liability insurance policy information, including coverage applicable to claims arising from the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office and Colorado County Jail.

item.title: TPIA Request: Jail Video Footage — January 19, 2026
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, March 10, 2026. Request submitted as PDF attachment with HIPAA authorization. Sent to Sheriff Justin Lindemann, Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens, and outside counsel J. Eric Magee and J. McVey of Allison, Bass & Magee.

March 10, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Jail Video Footage

Request submitted as PDF attachment: TPIA_Request_Jail_Video.pdf, accompanied by HIPAA Authorization for ████████████████.

item.title: TPIA Request: Official Electronic Communications
item.description:
Submitted via email to Colorado County, March 10, 2026. Request submitted as PDF attachment. Sent to County Judge Ty Prause, Sharon Marsalia, and the county IT department.

March 10, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Official Electronic Communications

Request submitted as PDF attachment: TPIA_Request_Official_Communications.pdf.

The request sought electronic communications — including emails, text messages, and other electronic records — between Colorado County officials relating to the January 19, 2026 incident at the Colorado County Jail involving ████████████████.

item.title: TPIA Request: Radio/Dispatch Audio and Medication Records — January 19, 2026
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, February 10, 2026. Acknowledged February 11, 2026 by Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens: “Your request is being reviewed.”

February 10, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Public Information Coordinator 400 Spring Street Columbus, TX 78934

RE: Texas Public Information Act Request

Dear Public Information Coordinator:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act (Texas Government Code Chapter 552), I am requesting on behalf of my wife, ████████████████, copies of the following records related to her (DOB: ██████████) during her incarceration at the Colorado County Jail from January 12, 2026 through January 21, 2026. ████████████████ is copied on this request.

item.title: TPIA Request: Dispatch Records and Call Logs — January 17 & 19, 2026
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, January 21, 2026. Acknowledged same day by Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens: “Request received and being reviewed.”

January 21, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Custodian of Records / Public Information Officer Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Dispatch Records and Call Logs

Dear Public Information Officer / Records Custodian:

Under the Texas Public Information Act (Texas Government Code, Chapter 552), I am requesting copies of the following records:

item.title: TPIA Request: Inmate Grievance Records — ████████████████
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, January 21, 2026. Acknowledged same day by Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens: “Request received and under review.”

January 21, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Custodian of Records / Public Information Officer Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — Inmate Grievance Records, ████████████████

Dear Public Information Officer / Records Custodian:

Under the Texas Public Information Act (Texas Government Code, Chapter 552), I am requesting copies of the following records from the Colorado County Jail:

item.title: TPIA Request: PREA Policies and Jail Operations Procedures
item.description:
Submitted via email to the Colorado County Sheriff’s Office, January 21, 2026. Acknowledged same day by Administrative Assistant Eugenia Behrens: “Request has been received and is under review.”

January 21, 2026

VIA EMAIL

Colorado County Sheriff’s Office Attn: Custodian of Records / Public Information Officer Columbus, TX 78934

Re: Public Information Request — PREA Policies and Jail Operations Procedures

Dear Public Information Officer / Records Custodian: (Please forward to the appropriate person if you aren’t the correct contacts for this request)
